# Claude Code prompt — [Fix] Card-header plant summary — group by equipment category

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-28.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

PR #241 fixed the card-header plant summary to render correctly (was showing `undefined (undefined× undefinedd)`), using the format `{equipmentDescription} ×{peakQty} · {equipmentDescription} ×{peakQty}` — grouping by equipment **description**, joined by `·`.

Owner's refinement: descriptions are too granular for the header. A card with two excavator variants (e.g. `Excavator 01T-03T` and `Excavator 03T-05T`) currently shows them as two separate entries, when they're really both Excavators. Owner wants grouping by equipment **category/type**, with variants stacked beneath the category line.

Picked layout (Option B from the brainstorm), with **per-variant duration** because durations differ between machine groups:

```
Plant
Excavators: 01T-03T 2 × 3d · 03T-05T 1 × 2d
Bobcats: 1 × 5d
Trucks: 6-tonne 2 × 1d
```

One line per category. Within each line, variants joined by `·`. Each variant entry shows `{variant} {peakQty} × {peakDays}d` — peak qty and peak days for that variant within the card. Category label is pluralised (Excavator → Excavators). If a variant has no distinguishing description (e.g. a generic "Bobcat" rate where description === category), the variant text is omitted: `Bobcats: 1 × 5d`.

The existing **Duration (days)** field on the card header (separately rendered) continues to show the overall card duration — `max(labourDuration, max(perCategoryPlantDuration))`. The per-variant durations in the Plant block are an additional breakdown, not a replacement.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `fix/plant-summary-group-by-category`.

## Data model — find or add `category`

Look at the `EstimatePlantRate` model in `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`. It likely already has a `category` field (the cutting / labour / plant / waste rate models tend to share this pattern). Confirm:

- If `category` exists: good. Use it. Verify seed data has sensible category values like `"Excavator"`, `"Bobcat"`, `"Truck"`, etc.
- If not: add a `category String?` field via auto-timestamped migration (`pnpm prisma migrate dev --name plant_rate_category`), and backfill the seed with appropriate categories. Acceptable categories from the existing rate set: Excavator, Bobcat / Skid-steer, Truck, Crane, Compactor, Loader, Forklift, Other. Set Other on any seeded rates that don't fit.

For each item's plant cluster, the rate's category is fetched alongside the existing description / qty / days when building the card summary.

## API — restructure `getCardSummary` response

Today's response:

```ts
plantSummary: Array<{ name: string; peakQty: number }>
```

Change to a category-grouped shape:

```ts
plantSummary: Array<{
  category: string;        // e.g. "Excavator", "Bobcat"
  items: Array<{
    variant: string | null;  // the distinguishing part of the description (e.g. "01T-03T (dry hire)"), or null if no variant text
    peakQty: number;
  }>;
}>
```

Server logic in `apps/api/src/modules/tendering/scope-of-works.service.ts` `getCardSummary`:

1. Collect all plant entries on items in the card (existing logic from PR #241).
2. Filter as today: skip entries with no `description`. Use `qty ?? 1` per the PR #241 fix-forward (matching pricing default).
3. Group by `rate.category`:
   - If category is null/missing, bucket under `"Other"`.
   - For each group, collect entries. Compute peak qty per **variant** (where variant = description with the category prefix stripped if the description starts with the category name).
4. Variant derivation:
   - If `description === category` (e.g. description = "Bobcat", category = "Bobcat"), variant = null.
   - If `description.startsWith(category + " ")`, variant = description with the prefix removed (e.g. `"Excavator 01T-03T (dry hire)"` → `"01T-03T (dry hire)"`).
   - Otherwise variant = full description.
5. Sort categories alphabetically; sort variants within each category alphabetically.

## Frontend — render multi-line

The card-header plant field currently renders the array as a single joined string via `formatPlantSummary()` in `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/scope-cards/utils/card-display.ts`.

Refactor:

1. Update the type in `useScopeCards.ts` to match the new server response shape.

2. Rewrite `formatPlantSummary()` to return an **array of category lines** (strings), one per category, OR a structured shape the renderer iterates:

```ts
formatPlantSummary(summary): string[] {
  if (!summary || summary.length === 0) return ["—"];
  return summary.map(group => {
    const label = pluralise(group.category);
    const variants = group.items
      .filter(it => it.peakQty > 0)
      .map(it => it.variant ? `${it.variant} ×${it.peakQty}` : `×${it.peakQty}`)
      .join(" · ");
    return `${label}: ${variants}`;
  });
}
```

3. In the card header (`ScopeCardsTab.tsx` or wherever the Plant field is rendered), iterate the returned string array and render each as its own `<div>` (or `<li>` in an unstyled list). Use the same typography as the existing single-line plant rendering — same font size, weight, colour. Tight line-height so the multi-line block doesn't push the card height too much.

4. Pluralisation helper — keep it simple:
   - Excavator → Excavators
   - Bobcat → Bobcats
   - Truck → Trucks
   - Crane → Cranes
   - Other → Other (no plural)
   - Fallback: append `s` to anything not ending in `s` already. (English plurals are imperfect but this set is small and Aussie-construction-specific; if owner adds new categories they can override the plural map.)

   Implement as a small lookup map in `card-display.ts` with a sensible fallback.

5. Em-dash fallback (`—`) when no plant entries at all — preserve existing behaviour.

## Tests

- API unit (`scope-of-works.service.spec.ts` or sibling): `getCardSummary` returns category-grouped plant summary with correct variant derivation. Cover:
  - Single excavator variant → `[{ category: "Excavator", items: [{ variant: "01T-03T (dry hire)", peakQty: 2 }] }]`
  - Two excavator variants → variants array has both, sorted alphabetically.
  - Bobcat with description matching category → `[{ category: "Bobcat", items: [{ variant: null, peakQty: 1 }] }]`
  - Mixed categories → both groups present, sorted alphabetically by category.
  - Null category on the rate → bucketed under "Other".

- Frontend unit (`card-display.test.ts`): `formatPlantSummary` returns array of lines matching Option B format. Cover:
  - Single category, single variant → one line like `"Excavators: 01T-03T (dry hire) ×2"`.
  - Single category, variant = null → `"Bobcats: ×1"`.
  - Multiple categories → multiple lines in returned array.
  - Empty summary → `["—"]`.
  - Pluralisation: Excavator → Excavators, Truck → Trucks, Crane → Cranes.

- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.

- Title: `[Fix] Card-header plant summary — group by equipment category`
- Body: describe the shape change (server response + frontend renderer), the Option B layout, and the pluralisation rule. Screenshot if possible showing before/after.

## Plant override — apply PR #239's edit/revert mechanic

The card header already has the override-highlight + ↺ revert mechanic on `peakCrew`, `totalPersonDays`, and `duration` (PR #239). The plant field should follow the same pattern.

PR #239's schema added `plantSummaryOverride String?` on `ScopeCard` (originally documented as JSON-encoded `[{ name, peakQty }]`). Use it as a **free-text override** instead — simpler UX, matches the user-has-final-say intent, and the new structured shape with duration would otherwise need a more complex JSON schema:

- When `plantSummaryOverride` is null: render the auto-derived multi-line category-grouped output (as specified above).
- When `plantSummaryOverride` is set: render the override string as-is. Wrap with the `OverrideField` component (same as the other card-header fields use) so it shows with `var(--surface-override)` tint + ↺ revert icon on hover.
- On click into the plant field: replace the multi-line rendered output with a single `<textarea>` populated with either the current override OR the auto-derived plain-text representation (the same string the renderer would produce, multi-line joined with `\n`). User edits freely.
- On blur: PATCH `plantSummaryOverride` with the new string. If the textarea is emptied, PATCH null to revert to auto-derived.
- On ↺ revert click: PATCH `plantSummaryOverride` to null. The field returns to auto-derived rendering.

Keep the textarea height proportional to the number of lines so it doesn't feel cramped while editing. Existing override styling pattern from the other card-header fields applies — tint, revert icon, hover affordance.

## Scope

Only the card-header plant summary rendering + override. Do not change the plant rate model beyond adding `category` if missing. Do not touch the quote PDF plant rendering or any other summary surface — if they share data shape with this endpoint, they inherit naturally; if not, they're out of scope for this PR.
